title: SIF-Bench Stage B case
description: One adversarial or benign-control case in a Stage B corpus. A case declares its expected security AND task outcome; a runner measures the current system against it without modification, and a scorer aggregates by family and severity. A case is never assumed to be blocked — the point is honest measurement.

input.description: Track-specific payload the runner applies to the current system.

expected_security_outcome.description: BLOCK: the control plane must refuse. ALLOW: a legitimate/benign control that must succeed. SURFACE_ABSENT: the attacked capability does not exist in v0.1, so there is nothing to exploit (this is not a security pass).

required_controls.description: Controls that, if present, should handle this case. Named for traceability; their absence is itself a finding.
